content.full_text:
Credit Cards                                    84210(g)+14)

CAMPA/GN HEADQUARTERS
1229 N HOLLYWOOD
N VIAS CALIFORNIA 92201

EL CAJON
POST OFFICE BOX 1171
EL CAJON, CALIFORNIA 92022
441-2411


                                        BOB WILSON
                            ASSEMBLYMAN, FORTIETH DISTRICT
                                    CHAIRMAN
                    ASSEMBLY GOVERNMENTAL ORGANIZATION COMMITTEE


                                February 25, 1977

                                                        A-77-523


Bob Stern
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Bob:

     I would like a written opinion to the following
question.

          Does the fact that a bank issues a credit
          card to a campaign constitute a loan to
          the campaign, especially if the balance
          owed on the charge card were not paid
          off within 30 days.

     I would appreciate your answer to this question
as soon as possible.

     Sincerely,
                                    Line of credit is reportable item to the
                                    campaign. Items charged are accrued expenses.
     BOB WILSON


                                                        123
     NOT PRINTED OR DISTRIBUTED PURSUANT

sections.question:
. 
Does the fact that a bank issues a credit 
card to a campaign constitute a loan to 
the campaign, especially if the balance 
owed on the charge card were not paid 
off within 30 days. 
I would appreciate your answer to this question 
as soon as possible.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether a bank-issued credit card provided to a campaign constitutes a loan under campaign finance law, particularly when the balance is not paid within 30 days.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from Bob Wilson to Bob Stern at the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION section present. The question section is clearly marked and extracted verbatim.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: . 
Does the fact that a bank issues a credit 
card to a campaign constitute a loan to 
the campaign, especially if the balance 
owed on the charge card were not paid 
off within 30 days. 
I would appreciate your answer to this question 
as soon as possible.

Does the fact that a bank issues a credit card to a campaign constitute a loan to the campaign, especially if the balance owed on the charge card were not paid off within 30 days. I would appreciate your answer to this question as soon as possible. Sincerely, Line of credit is reportable item to the campaign. Items charged are accrued expenses. BOB WILSON 123 NOT PRINTED OR DISTRIBUTED PURSUANT
embedding.summary: Bob Wilson requests a written FPPC opinion on whether a bank credit card issued to a campaign constitutes a loan, particularly when balances are not paid off within 30 days.
